Highest risk odor scenario. Shore gang likely required. Hospital clean after fishmeal is extreme.
Sweep + standard wash. Check for scale and oil residue from scrap.
Typically clean cargo. Standard sweep + rinse adequate.
Hardened mineral deposits may need acid descaler (phosphoric/citric). Neutralizer rinse after acid stage.
Soda ash (sodium carbonate) residues. Thorough wash. Check for any coating damage from previous hospital-clean demands.
Organic material. Check for insect infestation carefully. Standard wash protocol.
Mineral dust. Standard wash. Verify HME status of phosphate residues for MARPOL compliance.
CRITICAL WARNING: DO NOT WASH with water. Forms concrete-like layer if wet. Compressed air blow-out + sweep only. (Source: Isbester Ch.5). Shore gang mandatory for removal of any hardened deposits.
DO NOT WASH with water — forms concrete layer on hold sides. Compressed air blow-out and sweep only. Abrasive disc grinding required if water contact occurred.
Salt residues. Standard wash. If hold showing rust, apply limewash before next salt cargo (1 part Ca(OH)2 to 3 parts fresh water) to prevent discolouration.
Fine coal (>75% particles <5mm): both Group A (liquefaction risk) and Group B (chemical hazard) per IMSBC. Extra aggressive cleaning — same protocol as coal to grain but allow extra time.
